STATE OF CAUFORNIA 
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY COMMISSION 
1120 N STREET, P.O. BOX 1139 
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95805 
FRANCIS SARGUIS. Member 
Post Office Box 816 
Santa Barbara, CA 93102 
Deputy Clayton Roche 
Office of Attorney General 
Department of Justice 
Government Law Section 
June 22, 1976 
350 McAllister Street, Rm. 6000 
San Francisco, CA 
94102 
Re: 
Opinion Request 
Your No. CV 76/128 IL 
Dear Mr. Roche: 
EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor 
I am advised by Mr. Skjeie of the Sacramento Office of the Attorney 
General that my opinion request relating to the question of conflict 
of interest has been referred to you for research and writing. 
I know how loaded your office must be with opinion requests and other 
tasks. 
Bearing this in mind, I would still very much appreciate it 
if the opinion were issued as soon as possible, as it would greatly 
facilitate my work with the MTD. 
I would also like to add the following clarification: 
While my 
letter of May 26 addressed itself to "conflict of interest," I believe 
that the comments I made in that letter also raise by implication the 
issue of "compatibility of offices." Therefore, it would be appre- . 
ciated if you would specifically deal with both questions, and not 
just one. 
Sincerely, 
~JJ: 
I 
Francis Sarguis 
FS:cll 


STATE OF CAliFORNIA 
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY COMMISSION 
1120 N STREET, P.O. BOX 1139 
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95805 
Francis Sarguis, Commission Member 
P.O. Box 816 
Santa Barbara, CA 
93102 
May 26, 1976 
Office of Attorney General 
555 Capitol Mall 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
OPINION REQUEST 
CV 76/128 IL 
..xc HhOitt I E May CbIi6@iif. 04.... t-Lv-. ~4A.",: 
EDMUND G. BROWN 
Nearly two months ago, I was appointed by Governor Brown to the 
California Highway Commission. 
I am an attorney by occupation, sole practitioner, with a general 
practice. 
Among my various clients, I have one which is a public 
agency, to wit, the Santa Barbara Metropolitan Transit District 
(SBMTD) • 
I have rendered legal services to the SBMTD on an hourly basis, ever 
since the SBMTD was created some ten years ago. 
The District operates 
pursuant to the enabling legislation found under Public Utilities Code 
§§95000 et seq, and the nature of my legal services to the Board is 
more particularly described in §95533. 
I estimate that I render between 
8 and 15 hours of service per month to the SBMTD. 
My other legal work 
ranges over many areas, such as domestic relations, probate law, etc. 
As a result of my appointment to the State Highway Commission, one of 
the SBMTD Board members has asked whether my Commission appointment 
poses any conflict of interest problem with my legal work for the 
SBMTD. 
I personally considered this matter prior to receiving the 
appointment and carne to a negative conclusion. 
The same question was 
also discussed with Secretary Donald Burns of the Business and Trans-
portation Agency at the time he considered my appointment. 
He likewise 
came up with a negative conclusion. 
Lastly, since the matter was 
brought up by one of our SBMTD Board members, I have also raised 
question with Kingsley Hoegstedt, who is legal counsel for the Highway 
Commission. 
His conclusion was likewise negative. 
As Mr. Hoegstedt points out, I am not occupying an office with the 
SBMTD; in a manner of speaking, I am something akin to an "employee.
1i 


Office of Attorney General 
May 25, 1976 
Page two 
Thus, although I hold no "office" at the SBMTD, the Board would 
nevertheless like confirmation that no conflict exists. Accordingly, 
I wish to solicit from you a letter-opinion on this question. 
If I 
can be of any assistance in providing any other information which 
you deem pertinent to the inquiry, I am of course prepared to assist 
in any way I can. 
Sincerely, ~ 
..;1....., __ .. ' 
' 
~~ 
,~ 
Francis Sarguis 
FS:c11

Thus, although I hold no "office" at the SBMTD, the Board would

sections.conclusion: We conclude that no such conflict of interest arises from Mr. Sarguis' service on the Highway Commission with respect to his legal work for the Transit District. No public office or local government position is incompatible. If Mr. Sarguis is a consultant under the law, he is a public official subject to Government Code Section 87100 provisions, but there are no financial interests which would be materially affected by Transit District decisions. Mr. Sarguis' service as a consultant does not constitute a managerial decision of the Transit District by virtue of his Highway Commission service.
sections.question_synthetic: Does Mr. Sarguis' concurrent service as legal counsel to the Santa Barbara Metropolitan Transit District while serving on the California Highway Commission create a conflict of interest or compatibility of offices problem under Government Code Section 87100?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that Mr. Sarguis' concurrent service as legal counsel to the Transit District and member of the Highway Commission does not create a conflict of interest, as no financial interests of Mr. Sarguis would be materially affected by Transit District decisions.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: OCR quality issues throughout document affecting readability. Document includes both the incoming request letters (May 26 and June 22, 1976) and FPPC's response (August 17, 1976). The conclusion section is fragmented due to OCR errors but main legal conclusions are extractable. The FPPC notes it is not addressing whether Sarguis' Transit District consulting poses a conflict for his Highway Commission role—only the reverse.

QUESTION: with Kingsley Hoegstedt, who is legal counsel for the Highway 
Commission. 
His conclusion was likewise negative. 
As Mr. Hoegstedt points out, I am not occupying an office with the 
SBMTD; in a manner of speaking, I am something akin to an "employee.
1i 


Office of Attorney General 
May 25, 1976 
Page two 
Thus, although I hold no "office" at the SBMTD, the Board would 
nevertheless like confirmation that no conflict exists. Accordingly, 
I wish to solicit from you a letter-opinion on this question. 
If I 
can be of any assistance in providing any other information which 
you deem pertinent to the inquiry, I am of course prepared to assist 
in any way I can.

CONCLUSION: We conclude that no such conflict of interest arises from Mr. Sarguis' service on the Highway Commission with respect to his legal work for the Transit District. No public office or local government position is incompatible. If Mr. Sarguis is a consultant under the law, he is a public official subject to Government Code Section 87100 provisions, but there are no financial interests which would be materially affected by Transit District decisions. Mr. Sarguis' service as a consultant does not constitute a managerial decision of the Transit District by virtue of his Highway Commission service.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to an opinion request from Francis Sarguis regarding whether his service as legal counsel to the Santa Barbara Metropolitan Transit District is compatible with his appointment to the California Highway Commission. The FPPC advises that no conflict of interest exists.
